title: Current state of Fe-Mn-Al-C low density steels
abstract_summary: This review summarizes the physical metallurgy, processing, microstructural evolution, deformation mechanisms, and mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels. Al additions reduce density and Young’s modulus, and high Al and C lead to short-range ordering (SRO) and κ’-carbide precipitation, resulting in planar glide and new deformation mechanisms (MBIP, DSBR, SIP) in addition to TRIP and TWIP. The steels are classified into ferritic, ferrite-based duplex, austenite-based duplex, and austenitic types, with austenitic steels showing the most promise; however, reduced Young’s modulus and processing challenges limit automotive adoption, and future work should focus on alloying and processing strategies to improve stiffness.
research_objective: To provide a comprehensive review of the physical metallurgy, processing strategies, strengthening mechanisms, and mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels, and to identify key challenges and future directions for automotive applications.
material_systems: ferrite based duplex low density steels
austenite based duplex low density steels

key_sections.role: Background and motivation for low-density steels in automotive applications
Density and Young’s modulus dependence on Al content
Microstructure-based classification into ferritic, duplex, and austenitic types
Fe-Al, Fe-Mn-C, Fe-Al-C, and Fe-Mn-Al-C phase diagrams and alloy design principles
General processing, κ'-carbide and κ*-carbide precipitation, B2 precipitation, and microstructural evolution in ferritic, austenitic, and duplex steels
Strengthening mechanisms operating in ferritic, austenitic, and duplex low-density steels
Tensile behavior of ferrite-based, duplex, and austenitic steels under solution-treated, age-hardened, and non-age-hardenable conditions
Impact toughness, high strain rate behavior, fatigue, formability, corrosion resistance, and weldability
Evaluation of existing knowledge gaps and limitations in the literature
Proposed alloying and processing strategies and methods to increase Young’s modulus for automotive applications
Summary of key findings and outlook for Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels
